title: Selecting the Top N with Ties
notes: Top-N by rank, not by row count. DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY sal DESC) assigns rank 1 to the top salary, 2 to the next distinct salary, and so on with no gaps; filtering dr <= 5 keeps the five highest distinct salaries. Because two employees can share a salary (rank), the result can hold more than five rows. LIMIT 5 would arbitrarily cut a tied employee; DENSE_RANK keeps every employee at a qualifying salary.
